headline: Choose the Camp Dream GA path that matches how much planning support you want
subheadline: The pricing system now separates direct booking, guided planning, and custom retreat scoping so every visitor can move forward without confusion.
stepsHeadline: Each lane is built for a different kind of visitor

steps.title: Choose a Season Pass
steps.description: Best when you want a simple premium package with a direct path to purchase.

steps.title: Start a Family Adventure Weekend
steps.description: Best when you want a more curated family experience and support planning it well.

steps.title: Scope a Group Retreat
steps.description: Best when your school, church, or organization needs custom logistics and planning.

tiers.name: Free Resources
tiers.description: Browse articles, planning guides, and seasonal ideas before you commit to anything.

tiers.name: Season Pass
tiers.description: A direct-pay premium package for visitors ready to reserve a simple Camp Dream GA experience now.
features: Priority access to featured camp experiences

tiers.name: Family Adventure Weekend
tiers.description: A premium inquiry-first package for families who want a more curated experience and support.

tiers.name: Group Retreat Planning
tiers.description: For schools, churches, and organizations that need a custom path.

sections.title: Checkout only appears where it truly fits
sections.description: Direct checkout is reserved for simpler offers. Higher-touch experiences use inquiry-first paths so the premium experience stays aligned with the actual planning work.

sections.title: Premium support remains visible without becoming pushy
sections.description: Every tier points toward the next best action instead of forcing every visitor into the same funnel.

sections.title: Clear pricing supports both conversion and credibility
sections.description: Families and organizers can now understand the buying model quickly, which reduces hesitation and improves site quality.

faq.question: Why are some offers direct-pay and others inquiry-first?
faq.answer: Because simpler offers are easier to purchase immediately, while higher-touch experiences need planning and qualification first.

faq.question: Can I still talk to someone before I buy?
faq.answer: Yes. Contact and inquiry-first program pages are built for exactly that.

cta.heading: Use pricing when you want clarity fast, then move into the booking or planning path that fits.
cta.body: The page is meant to support confident action without crowding the experience with too many choices at once.
